headline: ECB holds rates steady at 2% amid euro strength concerns
analysis: ECB maintained all three key rates unchanged on Feb 5, keeping deposit rate at 2%. Policymakers noted euro appreciation concern but signal no imminent intervention. This hawkish hold pressures EUR/USD to test key resistance levels. Markets now expect ECB rates to remain around 2% through 2026 while Fed continues cutting toward 3% by year-end, widening rate differential in EUR's favor.

headline: Trump global tariffs create market volatility in Europe and crypto
analysis: Trump's 10% global tariffs announced this week triggered volatility across markets. European stocks ended mixed as investors assessed impact: Stoxx 600 swung between gains and losses, with corporate earnings offsetting tariff concerns. Tech sector particularly hit. Tariff uncertainty pressured Bitcoin below $63k this week before recovery bounce.

headline: Bitcoin bounces back to $68k after early-week capitulation
analysis: Bitcoin recovered sharply mid-week from $61k lows (down 30% for the week) to trade near $68.3k on Feb 26. Altcoins (SOL, DOGE, ADA, LINK) led the bounce with +10% gains. Failed test of $70k resistance signals ongoing consolidation amid tariff/geopolitical uncertainties. Crypto short squeeze supported recovery, but sentiment remains fragile.

headline: Gold hits $5,165/oz; JPMorgan bullish on 2026 outlook
analysis: Gold prices reached $5,165/oz on Feb 26 (up $2,289 YoY). JPMorgan raised long-term forecast to $4,500/oz while maintaining 2026 year-end target of $6,300/oz, signaling further upside. Geopolitical tensions (US-Iran talks in Geneva, Middle East concerns) and currency volatility driving safe-haven demand. Gold remains key hedge against inflation and FX volatility.

headline: S&P 500 earnings momentum expected to accelerate in 2026
analysis: Wall Street forecasts S&P 500 returns to crush 30-year average of 8.1% in 2026, driven by expected acceleration in corporate earnings growth. Week saw mixed earnings reports from Magnificent Seven companies (Alphabet, others), with some disappointments. Earnings season continues into March with potential for upside surprises as macro headwinds ease.

headline: Geopolitical tensions escalate: US-Iran nuclear talks ongoing in Geneva
analysis: Third round of US-Iran nuclear talks underway in Geneva amid rising Middle East tensions. Uncertainty around nuclear deal impacts energy prices and risk appetite. Oil prices sensitive to escalation. Broader geopolitical risk supports gold and volatility, pressures equities and growth assets. Market monitoring for any breakthrough or breakdown in negotiations.

headline: European earnings drive sentiment despite tariff headwinds
analysis: European corporate earnings season remains active through March. Stoxx 600 companies report mixed results as tariff uncertainty pressures margins. Banking sector benefits from ECB hold (maintains 2% deposit rate yields). Industrial and export-sensitive sectors face headwinds from Trump tariffs. Earnings beat/miss will determine market direction into March.
